note: jquery_ui_datepicker is a thin asset/library provider that re-supplies the jQuery UI Datepicker library after core removed it. It has no config UI, permissions, services, or PHP API, so all cases are `recipe` (response-graded), not `execution` (live-state).

evals.prompt: In a Drupal 11 module I have a render array and I want to attach the jQuery UI Datepicker asset library provided by the jquery_ui_datepicker module so the widget's JS/CSS load. Show the exact line to add.
What is the exact Drupal asset library identifier (module/library form) that the jquery_ui_datepicker module declares for the jQuery UI Datepicker component?
Why would I install the jquery_ui_datepicker contrib module on a Drupal 10 or 11 site? What problem does it solve?
evals.expected_output: jQuery UI was deprecated and progressively removed from Drupal core; this module re-provides the jQuery UI Datepicker asset library so legacy code that relies on it keeps working on Drupal 9.2+/10/11.

evals.prompt: Which Drupal module does jquery_ui_datepicker depend on to supply the underlying vendored jQuery UI files?
evals.expected_output: It depends on the base jquery_ui module (composer drupal/jquery_ui), which vendors the core jQuery UI files.

evals.prompt: In my theme's *.libraries.yml I want one of my theme libraries to pull in the jQuery UI Datepicker library from the jquery_ui_datepicker module as a dependency. What dependency string do I reference?
evals.expected_output: Reference jquery_ui_datepicker/datepicker under the dependencies: key of your theme/module library.

evals.prompt: I enabled jquery_ui_datepicker and I'm looking for its configuration/settings page and permissions to set up the datepicker. Where are they?
evals.expected_output: There are none — the module is a library-only compatibility shim with no config UI, permissions, services, or PHP API. You just enable it and attach the library.

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal module, how can I make the jQuery UI Datepicker library available site-wide by attaching it in a hook? Name the hook and show attaching the library.
